meta.title: India Geography Explorer — Competitive Exam Edition
meta.description: Drill-down map atlas: World ▸ India ▸ States ▸ Districts ▸ Popular locations, with demography, places, institutions, agriculture and wildlife at each level.
meta.exam_focus: UPSC, SSC, State PSC, Railways, Banking, Defence
meta.attribution: Boundaries: India district GeoJSON (udit-001) & Natural Earth (datasets/geo-countries), simplified.

properties.name: China
properties.note: Border is the Line of Actual Control (LAC), running through Ladakh, and along Arunachal/Sikkim.

properties.name: Bhutan
properties.note: Himalayan kingdom; close strategic ties with India. Capital Thimphu.

properties.name: Pakistan
properties.note: Capital Islamabad. Border via the Radcliffe Line; Wagah is the famous crossing.

properties.name: Myanmar
properties.note: Eastern neighbour, gateway to Southeast Asia. Capital Naypyidaw.

properties.name: Bangladesh
properties.note: India's longest land border (~4,096 km). Created in 1971. Capital Dhaka.

properties.name: Afghanistan
properties.note: Connected via PoK; border is the Durand Line. Capital Kabul.

properties.name: Nepal
properties.note: Open border with India; home to Mt. Everest (Sagarmatha). Capital Kathmandu.

properties.name: Sri Lanka
properties.note: Island nation across the Palk Strait. Capital Sri Jayawardenepura Kotte; Colombo is commercial capital.

data.summary: A world view centred on South Asia. India and its neighbours are outlined — click India to drill into states.
exam_facts: India shares land borders with 7 countries: Pakistan, Afghanistan, China, Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh and Myanmar.
Longest border is with Bangladesh (~4,096 km); the India–China border is the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
The Radcliffe Line separates India from Pakistan and Bangladesh; the Durand Line lies between Afghanistan and Pakistan.
Sri Lanka is separated from India by the Palk Strait and the Gulf of Mannar.

properties.name: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu

data.summary: The Republic of India — 28 states and 8 Union Territories. Click any state to view its profile; Uttar Pradesh drills further into districts.

popular_places.name: Sundarbans
popular_places.note: World's largest mangrove forest, West Bengal.

institutions.name: IIT (23 institutes)
institutions.note: Premier technical institutes; first at Kharagpur (1951).

agriculture.name: Rice & Wheat
agriculture.note: Staple foodgrains; India is among the top global producers.

agriculture.name: Green Revolution belt
agriculture.note: Punjab, Haryana, western UP — wheat & rice.

agriculture.name: Cotton
agriculture.note: Black soil (regur) of the Deccan — Maharashtra, Gujarat.

forests_wildlife.name: Western Ghats
forests_wildlife.note: UNESCO site; one of 4 hotspots in India.

exam_facts: India is the 7th largest country by area (~3.28 million km²) and most populous since 2023.
Mainland extends from 8°4'N to 37°6'N latitude and 68°7'E to 97°25'E longitude.
The Tropic of Cancer (23°30'N) passes through 8 Indian states.
Standard Meridian of India is 82°30'E, passing through Mirzapur (UP).
Largest state by area: Rajasthan; by population: Uttar Pradesh.
India has a coastline of ~7,516 km and shares borders with 7 nations.

data.summary: Uttar Pradesh — India's most populous state, 75 districts, capital Lucknow. Click a district to explore.

popular_places.name: Kashi Vishwanath (Varanasi)
popular_places.note: On the ghats of the Ganga.

popular_places.name: Sarnath
popular_places.note: Where Buddha gave his first sermon.

institutions.name: AMU, Aligarh
institutions.note: Founded by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan.

agriculture.name: Sugarcane
agriculture.note: India's largest producer — the doab and tarai belts.

agriculture.name: Wheat & Rice
agriculture.note: Green-Revolution western UP; fertile Gangetic plains.

exam_facts: Uttar Pradesh is the most populous state and would rank ~5th if it were a country.
It borders 9 states/UTs and the country of Nepal.
The Standard Meridian of India (82°30'E) passes through Mirzapur district.
Two major rivers — the Ganga and the Yamuna — meet at Prayagraj (Sangam).
UP sends the most members to the Lok Sabha (80 seats).

data.summary: Lucknow — the 'City of Nawabs' and capital of Uttar Pradesh, on the Gomti river.
exam_facts: Known for Awadhi cuisine, chikankari embroidery and Nawabi 'tehzeeb'.
The 1857 Siege of the Residency took place here.

data.summary: Built 1784 by Asaf-ud-Daula; the Bhulbhulaiya maze and a beamless central hall.
exam_facts: Built as a famine-relief project.
Houses the Asfi Mosque.

data.summary: 60-ft Awadhi gateway (1784), modelled on a gate in Constantinople.
Also called the Palace of Lights; built 1838 by Muhammad Ali Shah.
Ruins from the 1857 Siege of Lucknow.
Indian Institute of Management, established 1984.
Riverfront park along the Gomti.
Varanasi (Kashi / Banaras) — among the world's oldest continuously inhabited cities, on the Ganga.
exam_facts: Called the spiritual capital of India; the silk-weaving 'Banarasi saree' is GI-tagged.
Sarnath, where the Buddha first taught, lies nearby.

data.summary: One of the 12 Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva.
Main ghat, famous for the evening Ganga Aarti.
Central university founded 1916 by Madan Mohan Malaviya.
exam_facts: One of the largest residential universities in Asia.

data.summary: Where the Buddha delivered his first sermon; Dhamek Stupa.
The principal cremation ghat.
Agra — Mughal capital on the Yamuna, home to three UNESCO World Heritage Sites.

agriculture.name: Potato
agriculture.note: Part of UP's leading potato belt.

exam_facts: Three UNESCO sites: Taj Mahal, Agra Fort and nearby Fatehpur Sikri.
Famous for 'petha' sweet and marble inlay (pietra dura) handicraft.

data.summary: White-marble mausoleum built by Shah Jahan (c.1632–1653) for Mumtaz Mahal.
exam_facts: A UNESCO World Heritage Site (1983).
One of the New 7 Wonders of the World.

data.summary: Red-sandstone Mughal fort; UNESCO site (1983).
Akbar's planned capital; Buland Darwaza is the tallest gateway.
The 'Baby Taj', a precursor to the Taj Mahal.
Riverside garden aligned with the Taj across the Yamuna.
